data.title: NVIDIA (NVDA) Jumps Onto New Street's "2026 Best Ideas List" on Exceptional Order Momentum
data.description:
NVIDIA (NVDA) just received a major boost from New Street Research, as analyst Pierre Ferragu added the chipmaker to the 2026 Best Ideas list. Also, he reiterated a Buy rating on the stock, arguing that the scale of NVIDIA's current order momentum is unlike anything the tech industry has seen before.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Surge in Orders That Stunned Analysts

Ferragu points to comments made by CEO Jensen Huang at two separate events, one in late 2025 and another just a few months later. At the first event, Huang said NVIDIA had visibility into $500 billion worth of cumulative orders for its Blackwell platform and early Rubin ramps through 2026. 

But at the recent GTC keynote, Huang said that the same order outlook had jumped to $1 trillion. That means NVIDIA added about $500 billion in orders in less than five months, putting its current order run rate at more than $1.3 trillion a year.

Ferragu said he confirmed the numbers in one‑on‑one conversations with both Huang and CFO Colette Kress. He said Huang made it clear that NVIDIA already has over $1 trillion in orders today and expects that strong pace to continue.

New trading tool for NVDA bulls/bears

What It Means for 2026 and Beyond

The analyst believes the surge reflects two things: either 2026 orders have grown meaningfully since late 2025, or 2027 orders are already coming in strong.

Even if order growth slows between the Blackwell and Rubin product cycles, Ferragu expects NVIDIA to deliver a solid beat in 2026 and an even stronger one in 2027. His model now points to NVIDIA becoming a $1 trillion revenue company by the end of 2027, about double current consensus expectations.

If NVIDIA reaches $1 trillion in annual revenue, Ferragu estimates the company could earn $20–$25 in earnings per share and expects half of that to go toward buybacks and dividends.

Overall, with orders accelerating and long‑term visibility improving, the analyst said NVIDIA remains one of the strongest stories in tech. He added NVDA to his 2026 Best Ideas list, calling it a standout opportunity as demand for AI infrastructure continues to surge.

Is NVDA a Strong Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on NVIDIA stock based on 41 Buy and one Hold recommendations. The average NVDA stock price target of $274.03 indicates 53.29% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Netflix Stock (NASDAQ:NFLX) Slides Despite Plans to Focus on Originals
data.description:
Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) has by now likely learned a key lesson: content that you can only get in one place is what draws a lot of streaming viewer interest. Licensed programming is useful, sure, but originals are the big draw. And Netflix plans to ramp up its count of originals going forward significantly. However, investors seemed a bit less than pleased with the plan, as Netflix shares slid nearly 3% in the closing minutes of Thursday's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sequels and reboots—and of course, reimaginings—have been a big part of entertainment lately. While these are comparatively safe alternatives, they also require a certain quantity of truly original properties to support them, so that the next generation of sequels and reboots has fodder. And Netflix is eager to start finding that next generation, with plans to focus on "original storytelling" and also put more weight on the "theatrical comedy."

Film chairman Dan Lin noted, "We have a very healthy content budget. So if there's a great movie out there, we'll go out and either build it or acquire it." In fact, that plan to ramp up originals was part of why Netflix was in the hunt to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) for as long as it was, reports noted.

Dumbing it Down? Nope, Says Netflix

There were reports, not so long ago, that Netflix was actively requiring directors to repeat plot elements in dialogue, so as to make it easier for people whose attention was divided—being on their phones or the like—to follow content. But Netflix, again via Dan Lin, came out to say that that policy is simply not a thing.

"There is no such principle," Lin said, about as clear as could be without the use of flash cards. The concept came up once again at the Oscars, as Conan O'Brien and Sterling K. Brown re-enacted a scene from Casablanca, but over-explaining everything. Lin acknowledged that the bit was funny, but made it clear again that "…there's no such principle." Lin concluded, "I mean, if you watch our movies or TV shows, we don't repeat our plot. So I don't know where that comment came from. We are focused on making great movies."

Is Netflix Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 30 Buys and 10 Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 0.4% loss in its share price over the past year, the average NFLX price target of $114.35 per share implies 24.53% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Jeff Bezos Looks to Raise $100B for a New AI Fund
data.description:
Jeff Bezos, the founder of tech giant Amazon (AMZN), is in early talks to raise a $100 billion fund that will focus on buying manufacturing companies and using AI to speed up automation, according to The Wall Street Journal. In fact, Bezos has been meeting with major asset managers and traveling to regions such as the Middle East and Singapore to speak with sovereign wealth funds. Notably, the fund would target industries such as chipmaking, defense, and aerospace, and could make it one of the largest investment vehicles of its kind.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, this plan is closely connected to Project Prometheus, a startup for which Bezos now serves as co-CEO. Interestingly, the company is building AI systems that can simulate how the physical world works. This includes predicting how materials react under pressure or how airflow moves around objects. Bezos intends to use this technology to improve the efficiency of companies owned by the fund.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

Importantly, this move shows that AI is moving toward real-world industrial use rather than just software or chatbots. Indeed, companies are now investing heavily in AI for robotics, manufacturing, and physical systems. For example, Uber (UBER) founder Travis Kalanick is launching a manufacturing-focused AI venture, while Elon Musk is pushing robotics at Tesla (TSLA) with self-driving and humanoid projects.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $280 per share implies 34.7% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 'Don't Miss the Window,' Says Investor About Micron Stock Following Earnings
data.description:
Micron (NASDAQ:MU) kicked the doors off the hinges with its latest earnings report, surpassing already elevated expectations as demand for AI-driven memory continues to surge.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company posted fiscal Q2 revenue of $23.9 billion, up 196% year-over-year, while EPS reached $12.20 – well above the $1.56 it delivered in the same quarter last year and well ahead of Wall Street forecasts.

The momentum isn't slowing, either. Micron guided for ~$33.5 billion in revenue next quarter, far above consensus, as high-bandwidth memory demand tied to AI data centers continues to outpace supply.

And yet, despite the strong results and bullish outlook, the stock moved lower by 3.5% today, as investors appear to be looking past the headline numbers and focusing instead on the cyclical nature of the memory business or perhaps a potential AI bubble.

While some have suggested that the market needs some time to fully digest Micron's record-breaking numbers, investor Kenio Fontes takes a more assertive view, arguing that now is the time to lean in rather than hold back.

"This AI super-cycle is different, and 6x earnings is too cheap," emphasizes the investor.

Beyond the very solid quarter, Fontes argues that demand for Micron's products will be plenty stable. The investor points out that Micron is part-and-parcel of Nvidia's Vera Rubin, and this linkage will go a long way toward decreasing the company's cyclicity.

Moreover, Fontes argues that fears of an AI bubble are a bit misplaced. He cites the increasing adoption of AI agents, most recently demonstrated during Nvidia's GTC, and that much of this demand will go straight through Micron.

"The demand seems to me much more solid than what the market sees," Fontes added.

"The cherry on the cake for my Buy rating is the valuation," Fontes sums up, noting that the company guided for a non-GAAP EPS of $19.15 in the current quarter, which could translate into an EPS of $76 over the coming twelve months and a price-to-earnings ratio of close to 6x. (To watch Kenio Fontes' track record, click here)

Wall Street is optimistic as well. With 24 Buys and 2 Holds, MU enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $536.41 implies 19% upside from current levels. (See MU stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Fun Fact: Apple (AAPL) Is the Only Mag 7 Company Whose Capex Is Declining
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) is the only company among the so-called Magnificent 7 technology giants whose capital expenditures (capex) are declining.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In the fourth quarter of 2025, Apple's capex decreased 19%. That compares to a 95% year-over-year increase for Google parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) and an 89% annualized increase for Microsoft (MSFT).

Apple has been criticized for not having a more robust artificial intelligence (AI) strategy and those criticisms have weighed on the company's share price. But with concerns growing that the hyperscaler tech giants, which also include Amazon (AMZN) and Meta Platforms (META), are spending too much on AI, Apple looks prudent by comparison.

Apple's AI Revenue

However, while Apple might not be developing a dynamic AI strategy of its own, the company is still cashing in on the artificial intelligence boom. The iPhone maker stands to bring in more than $1 billion in App Store fees in 2026 from other companies' generative-AI apps, mostly ChatGPT, according to data from App Magic.

Apple's lucrative App Store model lets the consumer electronics giant profit from AI as a gatekeeper without having to spend billions of dollars to buildout the infrastructure needed to power the technology. AAPL stock has declined 8% this year.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

Apple stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 24 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy, nine Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $304.66 implies 22.35% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: SpaceX Gears Up for Its Most Important Rocket Test Yet
data.description:
Elon Musk's aerospace firm SpaceX is preparing for the planned April launch of its next-generation Starship V3, which will be one of its most important rocket tests yet. This new version is slightly larger than earlier models, with a taller Super Heavy booster and upper stage, along with upgraded Raptor 3 engines that produce more efficient and powerful thrust. At the same time, V3 is designed to carry over 100 tons to low Earth orbit, which is a big jump from about 35 tons previously.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This added capacity is critical for long-term goals such as missions to Mars, which require transporting large amounts of cargo, fuel, and, eventually, people. Separately, SpaceX is focusing on orbital refueling, which may be the most important upgrade of all. Instead of acting as a one-time-use rocket, Starship is being built as a full transportation system that can refuel in space and continue traveling deeper into the solar system. Without this capability, large-scale missions to the Moon or Mars would not be possible.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

The company is also aiming for full reusability of both the rocket and booster, which could reduce launch costs by up to 90%. If successful, this would open the door for larger science missions. Indeed, NASA expects Starship to be used for a mission to the Moon's South Pole in 2028, with the long-term goal of building a permanent research station there. Moreover, Elon Musk has said that the Moon may serve as a stepping stone before Mars, since it is much closer and easier to reach regularly.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 13 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $399.25 per share implies 5.1% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Get Ready for a $5.7 Trillion 'Triple-Witching' Event on Friday
data.description:
Friday may be a volatile day for the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), as $5.7 trillion worth of options tied to individual U.S. stocks, indexes, and exchange-traded funds (ETFs) are set to expire. The quarterly event, known as "triple-witching," is the largest March expiry since at least 1996, according to Citigroup.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Previous triple-witching occurrences have led to volatile moves as traders are forced to close, roll, or rebalance options positions.

Trade QQQ with leverage

Triple Witching Nears as Options Volume Jumps

Options trading in indexes and ETFs reached record notional volumes in March, according to Citi. The firm noted that Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN), Pinduoduo (PDD), and T. Rowe Price (TROW) could be subject to abnormal price action given that each stock has significant open interest in options that expire near current levels.

This quarter's triple-witching expiration comes amid an already volatile backdrop marked by the war in Iran, surging gas and oil prices, and plunging rate cut odds.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Morgan Stanley Has Named This Software Stock to Its 'Top Pick' List. Hint: It's Tax Season
data.description:
Wall Street investment bank Morgan Stanley (MS) has named Intuit (INTU), the software company behind TurboTax, to its "Top Pick" list.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Keith Weiss, a top five-star rated analyst, called Intuit a "durable compounder at a discount." In a note to clients, the Morgan Stanley analyst writes that it is "making Intuit our Top Pick as valuation screens attractive," adding that it sees "a path to top-line acceleration" for the company, whose other software products include QuickBooks for accounting and Credit Karma for personal finance.

Weiss argues that INTU stock offers an attractive entry point to investors with the share price down 28% this year and trading at about 20 times this year's earnings estimates. He adds that despite getting caught up in fears about AI's disruption of the software space, Intuit still offers long-term growth potential.

Intuit's Strong Numbers

He wrote that Intuit's valuation "looks even more attractive on a growth-adjusted basis," with the shares trading at a "1.01x CY26 PEG versus large-cap peer averages of 1.21x and the broader market >1.6x."

The bank also highlighted broad-based strength following Intuit's F2Q results, including "17% total revenue growth, 18% growth in Global Business Solutions, 21% Online Ecosystem growth, and approximately 40% growth in QBO Advanced and Intuit Enterprise Suite."

Weiss stated that these results reinforce confidence in the two key product cycles (Assisted Tax and Mid-Market Accounting & Services), which he believes can help move Intuit "back toward a 20% growth algorithm over time."

The firm stated that momentum in Assisted Tax is notable, with TurboTax growing "12% even with IRS filings tracking five points below prior-year levels" and customer visits exceeding five million by February 6.

Morgan Stanley said the April-quarter F3Q print represents the next major catalyst, offering "added clarity on the durability of TurboTax growth" and potential upward revisions to FY26 estimates.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) 'Could Double' by 2027, Says Five-Star Analyst
data.description:
The stock of chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) is woefully undervalued and could double in price by next year, says a top analyst at New Street Research.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pierre Ferragu, a five-star rated analyst with a 67% success rate and average return of 20%, has added NVDA stock to New Street Research's "Best Idea List for 2026," arguing that the stock could double in value by 2027.

Ferragu says that he's impressed with Nvidia's $1 trillion sales guidance for the company's Blackwell and Rubin microchips, but says that he feels the estimate should be higher and that CEO Jensen Huang is being conservative in his outlook.

Trade NVDA with leverage

Reasons to be Bullish on NVDA Stock

Ferragu adds that investors should pay attention to Nvidia's recently announced buyback strategy, which will see 50% of the company's free cash flow dedicated to stock buybacks and/or dividends, which will reward shareholders and are another reason to own the stock.

In a note to clients, Ferragu writes that "It all sounds like Nvidia could be a double bagger on the combination of earnings revision and the low multiple, opening the door to very efficient buybacks." Ferragu rates NVDA stock a Buy and forecasts a price target of $275, which is 55% higher than where the shares currently trade.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 41 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 40 Buy and one Hold recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $274.16 implies 53.32% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
